headline: Persistent memory systems surged this month as Microsoft Research and an open protocol pushed agent context management to the front.
body: The clearest rotation is toward persistent memory and context management: Microsoft Research introduced Memora, which separates storage from retrieval and uses a harmonic representation to balance abstraction and specificity, while the Open Memory Protocol proposed a single memory store spanning Claude, ChatGPT, and other assistants. Scaling moved in parallel with LongCat-2.0, a 1.6T-parameter mixture-of-experts model running 48B active parameters. Foundation-model defensibility surfaced as Wix-owned Base44 began rolling out its own model, and generative AI broke out on the consumer side with Gemini opening free personalized image generation to US users. Adversarial robustness and AI safety drew attention from a report that Meta posed as teenagers to probe rival chatbots with harmful prompts.

cited_items.title: Memora: A Harmonic Memory Representation Balancing Abstraction and Specificity
Open Memory Protocol – One Memory Store for Claude, ChatGPT, Curso
LongCat-2.0, a large-scale MoE model with 1.6T total and 48B Active
Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
Meta Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
